body: I see this dark theme / dark mode enhancement was closed and/or merged.  Has this been implemented in any way, even partially?  Or is it just scheduled to be?  If it's been implemented, are there any instructions or basic documentation for how to use it?  Thanks and sorry if I'm missing something.
author: kernelcrunch

body:
> I see this dark theme / dark mode enhancement was closed and/or merged. Has this been implemented in any way, even partially? Or is it just scheduled to be? If it's been implemented, are there any instructions or basic documentation for how to use it? Thanks and sorry if I'm missing something.

Hello! This was implemented and pushed to the master branch on May 21, but the latest stable release package was published on May 2, so it did not include this feature. However, if you go to https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/releases and download the "nightly" version, it definitely has a dark mode! (Though it is a pre-release version, so be cautious.)
author: chriscavalluzzi

body:
> Hello! This was implemented and pushed to the master branch on May 21, but the latest stable release package was published on May 2, so it did not include this feature. However, if you go to https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/releases and download the "nightly" version, it definitely has a dark mode! (Though it is a pre-release version, so be cautious.)

Excellent!  Thank you for the clarification.  I will cautiously use the pre-release for now.  (It looks great!)

Wondering how this action automatically changes below from

`  InstallerUrl: https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/releases/download/1.1.9/rustdesk-1.1.9-x64.exe`

to 
 
` InstallerUrl: https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/releases/download/1.1.10/rustdesk-1.1.10-x64.exe`

body:
I still do not know how to do 

> Please add a GitHub token with public_repo scope as a repository secret and rename the secret name in the workflow.

It is a pre-assigened token from you or any token I generate?
author: rustdesk

body:
> Do I have need to remove the version string from `rustdesk-1.1.10-x64.exe`?

No, you don't have to change anything in your release process.

> I still do not know how to do
> 
> > Please add a GitHub token with public_repo scope as a repository secret and rename the secret name in the workflow.
> 
> It is a pre-assigened token from you or any token I generate?

You've to goto https://github.com/settings/tokens/new and enter a token name (it can be anything, you want) and select `public_repo` in the permissions section. Refer to the screenshot below:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/83997633/173839262-42a1c827-8af2-4cb0-88fc-279a682c40c3.png)
After this, click generate token and copy it to the clipboard.
author: vedantmgoyal9

body:
> clipboard

Replace `${{ secrets.WINGET_TOKEN }}` with copied string?
author: rustdesk

body:
Now, after you've copied the token, visit https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/settings/secrets/actions/new and enter the information as shown in the below screenshot:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/83997633/173841062-d38bbd72-4894-4922-9bf5-8db5c1f852b6.png)
author: vedantmgoyal9

body:
> > clipboard
> 
> Replace `${{ secrets.WINGET_TOKEN }}` with copied string?

No, if you've done according to what's shown in the scrrenshot, you don't have to replace it.

author: sj6219

body:
> correction 2.
> 
> When you click the mouse button in two places on the screen while pressing the Command key on the Mac, the mouse input will be incorrect. I modified it.

Hey, I found an easier way to fix the mouse problem.

When the local Windows hits the win key, the remote macOS gets the command key. When you long press the win key, the command key will be continuously pressed and released. We only need to lock the state of the win key pressed to get the correct result.

> How far along is this? I would live to test it's compatibility with GLPI. We use RustDesk here at my job, and I have been setting up GLPI for inventory in IT and it would be cool to be able to remote in with one click.

if you apply the modifications in this pr, you get  "one click open" support.

This PR is kept draft because rustdesk is transitioning towards flutter, rendering this useless.

> @paspo what do I setup in GLPI for it to grab the RustDesk ID from each PC? I cant seem to get it to.

GLPI agent is OK (https://github.com/glpi-project/glpi-agent/pull/193)
GLPI is also ready (https://github.com/glpi-project/glpi/pull/12406)

body: Why do you have this fix? are you still using python2?

body: No, I'm using Python3. The problem is, if the code page of the terminal is 936 (which is the default value for zh-CN users), python will attempt to write to stdout using gbk (or gb2312?) codec, which introduces Unicode encode exception.
author: XieJiSS

body: Here's a blog post (in Chinese) explaining the issue: https://www.binss.me/blog/solve-problem-of-python3-raise-unicodeencodeerror-when-print-utf8-string/

> So flatpak can not be used as controlled side.

@rustdesk  
It might be possible to use RustDesk as controlled side via flatpak.  
At least this is possible for the AnyDesk flatpak (just tested it).  
https://flathub.org/apps/details/com.anydesk.Anydesk

So there might be a clue how to achieve this for RustDesk too.  
https://github.com/flathub/com.anydesk.Anydesk

Maybe this!?
https://github.com/flathub/com.anydesk.Anydesk/blob/16172333ff605f92ffcd9b6d0703c7b2faec0209/com.anydesk.Anydesk.json#L16
https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/sandbox-permissions-reference.html
author: kolAflash

body:
> > So flatpak can not be used as controlled side.
> 
> @rustdesk It might be possible to use RustDesk as controlled side via flatpak. At least this is possible for the AnyDesk flatpak (just tested it). https://flathub.org/apps/details/com.anydesk.Anydesk
> 
> So there might be a clue how to achieve this for RustDesk too. https://github.com/flathub/com.anydesk.Anydesk
> 
> Maybe this!? https://github.com/flathub/com.anydesk.Anydesk/blob/16172333ff605f92ffcd9b6d0703c7b2faec0209/com.anydesk.Anydesk.json#L16 https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/sandbox-permissions-reference.html

I've tested `Anydesk`, it does not have an daemon service, so it cannot be invoked when the `Anydesk` is closed. `RustDesk` aims to handle this feature, so we need a small and light daemon service to run forever in the background, which gives `RustDesk` ability to automatically open when a new connection is comming. We assume that flatpak does not support daemon service currently due to [this](https://flatpak.org/faq/#Can_Flatpak_be_used_on_servers_too_), it lacks [Service Management](https://snapcraft.io/docs/service-management) that `snap` contains.
author: Kingtous

body:
@Kingtous  
> I've tested `Anydesk`, it does not have an daemon service, so it cannot be invoked when the `Anydesk` is closed. `RustDesk` aims to handle this feature, so we need a small and light daemon service to run forever in the background, which gives `RustDesk` ability to automatically open when a new connection is comming.

Sure, a background daemon is something else.

But in principal running RustDesk via FlatPack should should be possible for the controlled side. It's just necessary that the RustDesk application runs in "foreground" and not only as a daemon.

 

> So flatpak can not be used as controlled side.

Maybe I got @rustdesk comment wrong. I just wanted to say, that it's possible to control a desktop from an application inside FlatPack in general.
author: kolAflash

body: Other projects do this by staying in the tray, which seems to be a viable solution to this problem until daemon support lands on flatpack (if ever).
author: Root-Core

body:
Is there a way to run the nightly flatpak image in the background? For example like `flatpak run com.rustdesk.RustDesk --background`. I was able to create a autostart file doing the following steps. But now every time my computer starts the rustdesk windows pops up.

```
$ nano ~/.config/systemd/user/rustdesk.service

[Unit]
Description=Remote Desktop
StartLimitIntervalSec=500
StartLimitBurst=5

[Service]
ExecStart=flatpak run com.rustdesk.RustDesk
ExecStop=flatpak kill com.rustdesk.RustDesk
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5s

[Install]
WantedBy=graphical-session.target

$ systemctl --user enable rustdesk
```

body: The locale code for the catalan language in Spain is ca_ES
author: sstepansky

body:
> The locale code for the catalan language in Spain is ca_ES

According to  [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catalonia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catalonia)
the ISO 3166 code is ES-CT. I used underscore because of naming consistency.
author: flusheDData

body: Thank you for your help. Changing name.
author: flusheDData

body:
> > The locale code for the catalan language in Spain is ca_ES
> 
> According to  [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catalonia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catalonia)
> the ISO 3166 code is ES-CT. I used underscore because of naming consistency. 

That code is for the name of the region, not the language.
https://www.localeplanet.com/icu/ca-ES/index.html

ca_ES is the proper code for the locale. Compare with pt_PT (already in the repo) and pt_BR for example. These distinguish the varieties of Portuguese language spoken in Portugal and Brazil, respectively.

body:
This scroll problem seems to a universal problem when using **track pad**, unfortunately I don't have a good device for windows develop, so I can't fix this problem for windows currently. But this patch is enough for fix mac itself.

Here is all test cases

- [x] Mac control mac (mouse, track pad)
- [x] Ios control mac (gesture) 
- [x] Win control mac (mouse)
- [x] Mac control win (mouse)

- [ ] Win control mac (track pad)
- [ ] Mac control win (track pad)

So there will need a follow patch to distinguish track pad input from mouse input, and send track pad input on win (linux may also needs support).

body: I experiment with base64 encoding to support all future ID strings, because base64 is already a dependency. So far it works, whats missing is backwards compatibility. We need a way to tell if a filename is Base64 encoded or not (and if not, then convert it once). Maybe a special char at the start of the filename indicating that it's encoded?
author: biberino

body:
> I experiment with base64 encoding to support all future ID strings, because base64 is already a dependency. So far it works, whats missing is backwards compatibility. We need a way to tell if a filename is Base64 encoded or not (and if not, then convert it once). Maybe a special char at the start of the filename indicating that it's encoded?

Good job, you can add a long prefix something like "base64_". But for human friendly, please only encode if there is special char.
author: rustdesk

body: Or you can decode first, if fail to decode, then it is not base64.
